kings_guild.name: The King's Guild
kings_guild.description: Master artisans of the King's Guild each ply a different trade in a different quarter of the city. Work out who's who.
clues: The artisan serving the Sheriff's levy is immediately below Godfrey.
More members of the River Ward are in the Joiners' Guild than members of the Hill Ward.
The artisan serving the Prince's levy is exactly 4 coins more than the artisan with the Saw.
The Blacksmith's levy is between Beatrix and the artisan serving the Prince.
Kingsford goes with none of Fletcher, Potter, or Mallet.
Potter, Loom, and Mallet go with Beatrix, Godfrey, and Rowan, in some order.
Exactly one of these is true: Beatrix goes with Loom; Rowan goes with Fletcher; Rowan goes with Prince.
The artisan in Oldwall's levy is lower than both Rowan and the artisan serving the Prince.
Godfrey goes with at least one of Carpenter or Sheriff.
15 coins goes with at most one of Fletcher, Saw, and Countess.
Mallet goes with exactly two of Rowan, Potter, and 15 coins.

Suppose Aldric went with Loom — following the clues from there hits a contradiction, so it doesn't.
chain: Start by assuming Aldric goes with Loom.
Loom goes with Aldric, so Beatrix can't go with Loom.
Loom goes with Aldric, so Godfrey can't go with Loom.
Loom goes with Aldric, so Rowan can't go with Loom.
…but now the clue “Potter, Loom, and Mallet go with Beatrix, Godfrey, and Rowan, in some order” can no longer hold — a contradiction.
So Aldric does not go with Loom.

fishing.name: The Fishing Derby
fishing.description: Anglers at the lake-country derby each landed a different fish from a different pond on a different lure. Work out who caught what, and where.
clues: The angler fishing with Nightcrawler's weight is immediately below Mabel.
More members of the North Watershed are in the Sunfish Family than members of the East Watershed.
The angler fishing with Maggot's weight is exactly 4 lb more than the angler using the Swimbait.
The angler who landed the Bass' weight is between Esther and the angler fishing with Maggot.
Foxglen goes with none of Pike, Trout, or Spoon.
Trout, Spinnerbait, and Spoon go with Esther, Mabel, and Wade, in some order.
Exactly one of these is true: Esther goes with Spinnerbait; Wade goes with Pike; Wade goes with Maggot.
The angler at Stillwater's weight is lower than both Wade and the angler fishing with Maggot.
Mabel goes with at least one of Catfish or Nightcrawler.
8 lb goes with at most one of Pike, Swimbait, and Corn.
Spoon goes with exactly two of Wade, Trout, and 8 lb.
